# Transcrição: WFF2019_M8_L06-2_Bonuses_for_Joint_Venture_Webinar_Hosts_MP4

- **Duração:** 08:53

## [00:00 - 02:59]

Hi, this is Don Crowther. Let's talk about a strategy that does a great job of adding to sales when you're doing a JV webinar. So that sales technique is to have your host, the JV partner, offer an extra bonus to everyone who buys. So you've got your list of bonuses that you're offering on that webinar, but then they put another bonus or two on top of that specifically designed to get their list to buy your product. Now, normally you'll only do this when you only have just that JV's invitees on the webinar. If you ever, you possibly could do it with a couple of JV's, but you're going to need to make it clear to everyone, both the JV partners as well as the people are buying that everyone who buys on that webinar will get all the bonuses offered by all the JV partners on that particular webinar. So when do you do this in the webinar? One option is, and frankly, this is where I recommend you do it. It's after you finish making the offer before you start answering questions. So I've given the offer, I've given all my bonuses and everything. And just before I go into the FAQ section, which opens up the question and answer part, I have the JV partners step in and offer their set of bonuses at that particular point in time. But you can put it in wherever it fits the best. You need to make sure that your host understands that the richer the bonus they provide, the more money they'll probably earn here. So their bonus, by giving this bonus, they show support of your product in a very tangible way. And it becomes a recommendation that their people, their list, should buy this product. So the psychology here is Bob, who sent me the email saying, because I'm on his list, saying I should come watch this webinar that Don's producing. Bob, who I already trust, is telling me I should buy this product. In fact, he feels so strongly about it that he's actually putting in a bonus to get me to do it. That must mean that Bob, who I already trust, really likes Don's product, who I don't know or trust yet. And so I'm willing to take a leap of faith because I trust Bob. And because he gave that bonus to go ahead and buy Don's product. So I recommend that you have the host when you're at this stage in the webinar, come on the air and say something like this. I'm a huge supporter of your name and the work that he or she is doing. And I recommend that you buy this product. In fact, I feel so strongly about that that I'm going to offer a special bonus to everyone who buys it from this webinar, and then they go on and describe that bonus.

## [03:00 - 06:00]

Now there's some psychology that's going on here. So they're saying, I'm a huge supporter. I recommend you buy this product. Now that's something that goes one step beyond what most joint venture kinds of relationships do. They imply that you should buy that. We're putting one more piece in here, which is they're saying, I recommend you buy it. And then on top of that, I feel so strongly that this will be good for you, that I'm giving you a bonus to incentivize you to actually do that step and buy. So what they're basically doing here is they're pushing their trust onto your product so that their audience who already trusts you, trust them, will buy your product, even though they don't know or trust you very well yet. Then what I do is I have them prepare a couple of slides describing their bonus and share. And so at the point that they're coming in to take over, I actually have them share their screen so their people can see them. And I actually have them do it in a look that's different than mine. In fact, I'd really like it when they have a signature look that their audience already recognizes. And they go, oh, this isn't just shoved into Don's presentation. This is in Bob's, this is Bob's presentation style. I know that I trust that. Oh, okay. I'm very interested. Then once they're done with all that, I then acknowledge and praise and thank them for this generous bonus. You know, like, wow, that's an amazing bonus. And then I reiterate that they're giving the bonus because they believe that you should buy. Then I clarify on air that this bonus applies to anyone who buys before the cart closes. So what I do is I actually, I'm having this conversation with Bob. And so I actually say, so Bob, I just want to make sure this bonus is valid for anyone who buys before Monday night at midnight when this cart, when the cart closes, right? And so then Bob says, yes. So what that does is it again solidifies in their audience's mind. Hey, this is a joint thing between Don and Bob. They know each other. They trust each other. And so really Bob is really in this. And he really does like this. So that little extra piece of conversation in there does a huge amount to reinforce all the psychology I've been describing. And then every time after that point that I refer to my bonuses, I also refer to their bonus also. And I make sure that it's clear that their bonus that they offer is now part of my offer.

## [06:01 - 08:53]

And I actually, after this point, I include their bonus in all the emails that go out after this to this list that we've been promoting to. So Bob sent some traffic that's registered for this webinar. And so I've isolated those people into a separate list. And I'm only mailing to them about this particular webinar. And so then everything that happens after this in the webinar funnel, I'm only mailing to that group of people that came from Bob in the first place. And so they're getting those emails. And in those emails, now I am also adding, hey, Bob put an extra bonus on this product. And so I'm talking about that very specifically again, so that I can get Bob's trust and put it onto my product. Now that last point actually brings up something. I actually like to create a separate email campaign and separate pages for each JV partner. I basically take my main one, my main campaign, my main pages, I duplicate them, and then I add in their information. So I'll put a picture of Bob in it. I'll put, hey, fans of Bob, those kinds of things. So that it's very clear that even though the email is coming from me, and even though these pages are my pages, that they're getting it because they are followers of Bob. And I do all this for a couple of reasons. The first one is so I can talk about them in the promotional sequence. But also, and this is somewhat important, I do it so that I don't make a mistake and we forget to replace that content in my main sequence. So in the past, I've had a couple of times where I just took my main sequence and I just put in some references to Bob in it, and I send it out saying, Oh, I'll just change it back as soon as I'm done. And then I forget to change it back. And so then people are starting to get emails and they're sending me emails and saying, who's Bob? I have no idea what's going on here. They get confused. Confusion never helps sales. Then how do you go about having Bob actually deliver his bonus? So what I do and what I recommend you do is after your card is closed, you send Bob a list of the people who bought the offer, and then Bob fulfills his own part of the bonus. So I fulfill my product and my bonuses. Bob takes care of getting his bonuses to that group of people that don't have to worry about putting Bob's bonus in my portal and having it only go to Bob's people and all those other kinds of things like that. Just let Bob deal with that himself by giving him a list of the people who bought and qualified for his bonus.
